5414	  \]
5415	  sends an \(S\)-scheme \(T \to S\) to the set of equivalence classes
5416	  \(\langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle\) of pairs \((\mathcal{F}, q)\) where
5417	  \begin{enumerate}
5418	    \item \(\mathcal{F}\) is a coherent sheaf on \(X_T = X \times_S T\) whose
5419	      schematic support is proper over \(T\) (\cref{def:has_proper_support}) and
5420	      which is flat over \(T\);
5421	    \item \(q : E_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) is a surjective
5422	      \(\mathcal{O}_{X_T}\)-linear homomorphism, where \(E_T\) is the pull-back
5423	      of \(E\) along \(X_T \to X\);
5424	    \item for every \(t \in T\) the Hilbert polynomial of \(\mathcal{F}|_{X_t}\)
5425	      with respect to \(L|_{X_t}\) (\cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}) equals
5426	      \(\Phi\).
5427	  \end{enumerate}
5428	  Two pairs \((\mathcal{F}, q)\) and \((\mathcal{F}', q')\) are equivalent iff
5429	  \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\). On morphisms \(f : T' \to T\) over \(S\), the functor
5430	  acts by pulling back the quotient along \(X_{T'} \to X_T\) (well-defined
5431	  since tensor product is right-exact and preserves both flatness and
5432	  properness). The Hilbert scheme is the special case
5433	  \(\Hilb^{\Phi,L}_{X/S} := \Quot^{\Phi,L}_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}\).
5434	\end{definition}
5435	
5436	\section{The Grassmannian scheme}
5437	\label{sec:grassmannian_scheme}
5438	
5439	The Quot construction proceeds by embedding \(\Quot^{\Phi,L}_{E/X/S}\) as a
5440	locally closed subfunctor of a Grassmannian over \(S\). Since Mathlib carries
5441	no Grassmannian \emph{scheme} (only a finite-rank-quotient variety in
5442	linear algebra), we package the construction here.
5443	
5444	\begin{definition}\leanok
5445	[Grassmannian scheme]
5446	  \label{def:grassmannian_scheme}
5447	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian}
5448	  \uses{def:quot_functor, def:is_locally_free_of_rank}
5449	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, "Elementary Examples, Exercises~(2)" and
5450	  % "Construction of Grassmannian" (read from
5451	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L546-L599
5452	  % + L773-L850).
5453	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
5454	  % "For any integers \(r\ge d\ge 1\), an explicit construction
5455	  %  the Grassmannian scheme
5456	  %  \(\grass(r,d)\) over \(\Z\), together with the tautological
5457	  %  quotient \(u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U\) where \
5458	  %  \(\U\) is a rank \(d\) locally free sheaf on \(\grass(r,d)\),
5459	  %  has been given at the end of this section. [...]
5460	  %  Show that \(\grass(r,d)\) together with the quotient
5461	  %  \(u:\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U\) represents the
5462	  %  contravariant functor
5463	  %  \(\)\Grass(r,d) = \Quot_{\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\Z}/\Z/\Z}^{d, {\mathcal O}_{\Z}}\(\)
5464	  %  from schemes to sets, which associates to any \(T\) the
5465	  %  set of all equivalence classes
5466	  %  \(\langle \F, q\rangle\) of quotients \(q: \oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_T \to \F\)
5467	  %  where \(\F\) is a locally free sheaf on \(T\) of rank \(d\).
5468	  %  [...]
5469	  %  Using the above show that, more generally, if \(S\) is a scheme and
5470	  %  \(E\) is a locally free \({\mathcal O}_S\)-module of rank \(r\),
5471	  %  the functor \(\Grass(E,d)=\Quot_{E/S/S}^{d,{\mathcal O}_S}\)
5472	  %  on all \(S\)-schemes which
5473	  %  by definition associates to any \(T\)
5474	  %  the set of all equivalence classes \(\langle \F, q\rangle\) of quotients
5475	  %  \(q: E_T \to \F\) where \(\F\) is a locally free sheaf on \(T\) of rank \(d\),
5476	  %  is representable. The representing scheme
5477	  %  is denoted by \(\grass(E,d)\) and is called the rank \(d\)
5478	  %  relative Grassmannian of \(E\) over \(S\)."
5479	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1, Exercise (2) and "Construction of
5480	  Grassmannian" (FGA Explained Ch.~5).}
5481	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme and let \(V\) be a locally free
5482	  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module of rank \(r\). For an integer \(1 \le d \le r\), the
5483	  \emph{Grassmannian of rank-\(d\) quotients of \(V\)} is the functor
5484	  \[
5485	    \mathrm{Grass}(V, d) : (\mathrm{Sch}/S)^{op} \longrightarrow \mathbf{Set},
5486	    \qquad
5487	    T \longmapsto \{ \langle \mathcal{F}, q\rangle :
5488	    q : V_T \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F},\; \mathcal{F}
5489	    \text{ locally free of rank } d \}
5490	  \]
5491	  with equivalence \(\ker(q) = \ker(q')\); the rank-\(d\) local freeness of
5492	  \(\mathcal{F}\) is as in \cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank}. Concretely,
5493	  \(\mathrm{Grass}(V, d) = \Quot^{d,\mathcal{O}_S}_{V/S/S}\): the Quot functor
5494	  (\cref{def:quot_functor}) in the special case \(X = S\), \(E = V\), constant
5495	  Hilbert polynomial \(\Phi = d\).
5496	\end{definition}

5498	\begin{lemma}[Representability predicate]
5499	  \label{lem:functor_is_representable_mathlib}
5500	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Functor.IsRepresentable}
5501	  \mathlibok
5502	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
5503	  Let \(\mathcal{C}\) be a category and \(F : \mathcal{C}^{op} \to \mathbf{Set}\)
5504	  a presheaf of sets. The predicate \(F.\mathrm{IsRepresentable}\) asserts that
5505	  \(F\) is representable, i.e.\ that there exists an object \(Y\) of
5506	  \(\mathcal{C}\) together with a natural bijection
5507	  \(\mathrm{Hom}_{\mathcal{C}}(-, Y) \cong F\) (the Yoneda bijection).
5508	\end{lemma}

5567	  Let \(S\) be a noetherian scheme, \(V\) a locally free \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-module
5568	  of rank \(r\), and \(1 \le d \le r\). The functor \(\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)\) of
5569	  \cref{def:grassmannian_scheme} is representable by a smooth projective
5570	  \(S\)-scheme \(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d) \to S\) of relative dimension \(d(r-d)\),
5571	  equipped with a tautological quotient
5572	  \(\pi^* V \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\) of rank \(d\). The determinant
5573	  line bundle \(\det(\mathcal{U})\) is relatively very ample, and the
5574	  associated linear system gives a closed embedding
5575	  \(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d) \hookrightarrow
5576	  \mathbb{P}_S\!\left(\bigwedge^d V\right)\) -- the \emph{Pl\"ucker embedding}.
5577	
5578	\end{theorem}

5580	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: see the "Construction of Grassmannian" sub-section of
5581	% [Nitsure], \S 1 (references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
5582	% L773-L930), which gives the construction in five named steps
5583	% ("Construction by gluing together affine patches", "Separatedness",
5584	% "Properness", "Universal quotient", "Projective embedding"). The total
5585	% verbatim text is ~150 lines; it is reproduced piecewise in the
5586	% SOURCE QUOTE comment of the theorem statement above.
5587	\begin{proof}
5588	  
5589	  Over \(S = \Spec \mathbb{Z}\) and \(V = \mathcal{O}_S^{\oplus r}\) the
5590	  Grassmannian \(\mathrm{Gr}(r, d)\) is constructed by gluing
5591	  \(\binom{r}{d}\) affine charts \(U^I = \Spec\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) indexed by
5592	  size-\(d\) subsets \(I \subseteq \{1, \dots, r\}\). The chart \(U^I\)
5593	  carries the matrix \(X^I\) of size \(d \times r\) whose \(I\)-th \(d \times d\)
5594	  minor is the identity, with the remaining \(d(r-d)\) entries free
5595	  variables (so \(U^I \cong \mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_{\mathbb{Z}}\)). The
5596	  transition maps \(\theta_{I,J} : U^I \cap U^J \to U^J \cap U^I\) defined
5597	  by \(X^J \mapsto (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\) on the locus where \(\det X^I_J\) is
5598	  invertible satisfy the cocycle condition
5599	  \(\theta_{I,K} = \theta_{I,J} \circ \theta_{J,K}\). The glued
5600	  \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec\mathbb{Z}\) is smooth of relative dimension
5601	  \(d(r-d)\). Separatedness is checked from the diagonal
5602	  \(\Delta_{I,J} \subset U^I \times U^J\) cut out by \(X^J_I X^I - X^J = 0\).
5603	  Properness follows from the valuative criterion for DVRs: given
5604	  \(\varphi : \Spec K \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) over a DVR \(R\) with fraction
5605	  field \(K\), factoring through some chart \(U^I\) via a ring map
5606	  \(f : \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to K\), choose \(J\) minimising the valuation
5607	  \(\nu(f(P^I_J))\) of the minor; on the chart \(U^J\) all entries have
5608	  non-negative valuation, so \(f\) extends uniquely to \(\Spec R\).
5609	
5610	  The universal quotient \(u : \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\oplus r}
5611	  \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\) is given on \(U^I\) by the matrix \(X^I\)
5612	  and glued by \(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1} \in GL_d(U^I \cap U^J)\). The
5613	  determinant line bundle \(\det\mathcal{U}\) has transition functions
5614	  \(\det g_{I,J} = 1/P^I_J\); the global sections
5615	  \(\sigma_I \in \Gamma(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d), \det\mathcal{U})\) defined by
5616	  \(\sigma_I|_{U^J} = P^J_I\) form a base-point-free linear system separating
5617	  points over \(\Spec\mathbb{Z}\), giving an embedding into
5618	  \(\mathbb{P}^{\binom{r}{d}-1}_{\mathbb{Z}}\), which is closed by
5619	  properness. Representability of \(\mathrm{Grass}(r,d)\) is then verified
5620	  on the universal pair \((\mathrm{Gr}(r,d), u)\): a quotient
5621	  \(q : \mathcal{O}_T^{\oplus r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{F}\) of
5622	  rank \(d\) on \(T\) is everywhere locally trivialised; on the locus where
5623	  the columns indexed by \(I\) are a basis the matrix of \(q\) has the form
5624	  \(X^I\), defining the classifying morphism \(T \to U^I \subset
5625	  \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\). Base change from \(\mathbb{Z}\) to a general noetherian
5626	  base \(S\) with a vector bundle \(V\) is direct: trivialise \(V\) on an open
5627	  cover and glue. For non-trivial \(V\) the same construction produces
5628	  \(\mathrm{Gr}_S(V, d)\) with Pl\"ucker embedding into
5629	  \(\mathbb{P}_S(\bigwedge^d V)\).
5630	\end{proof}

5632	\section{Out of scope}
5633	\label{sec:quot_out_of_scope}
5634	
5635	This chapter packages \emph{only} the Quot-foundations layer: the Hilbert
5636	polynomial, the Quot functor, and the Grassmannian scheme with its
5637	representability. The following are explicitly out of scope here:
5638	\begin{itemize}
5639	  \item \textbf{Representability of the Quot scheme} -- the
5640	    Grothendieck--Altman--Kleiman existence theorem and its proof skeleton
5641	    (boundedness, Grassmannian embedding, flattening stratification,
5642	    valuative criterion) are not constructed in this chapter.
5643	  \item \textbf{Flat base change of cohomology} (Stacks 02KH) and its
5644	    associated prerequisites -- not constructed in this chapter.
5645	  \item \textbf{Hilbert scheme as a separate chapter}. The Hilbert scheme
5646	    \(\Hilb_{X/S}\) is the special case
5647	    \(\Hilb_{X/S} = \Quot_{\mathcal{O}_X/X/S}\) of the Quot functor and is
5648	    introduced inline (\cref{def:quot_functor}); no separate chapter.
5649	  \item \textbf{Snapper's Lemma} -- the polynomial-eventually property of
5650	    Euler characteristics, used in \cref{def:hilbert_polynomial}. Cited
5651	    via Nitsure \S 1 and (textbook reference) Hartshorne III.5.2;
5652	    Lean formalization is a prerequisite sub-task.
5653	  \item \textbf{Identification with \(\mathbb{P}^n\) and relative
5654	    Grassmannian variants} (Nitsure \S 1, Exercises (1)--(4)) --
5655	    optional applications, not consumed by Route A.
5656	  \item \textbf{Group-scheme structure on \(\Pic_{C/k}\)} (A.2.c) -- the
5657	    abelian-group-scheme refinement is downstream. Its blueprint home is the
5658	    FGA Picard-representability assembly (the chapter that bundles the relative
5659	    Picard functor, its representability, and the abelian-group-scheme
5660	    structure on \(\Pic_{C/k}\)); that assembly lives in the parent project
5661	    which consumes this Quot-foundations layer and is not part of the present
5662	    subproject.
5663	  \item \textbf{Identity-component \(\Pic^0\) and abelian-variety
5664	    structure} (A.3) -- a separate Route A sub-row, fed by but not
5665	    constructed in this chapter.
5666	\end{itemize}

2823	  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_eUnit,
2824	        lem:base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_pushforwardComp,
2825	        lem:base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_pullbackComp,

2829	  The four distributed \(\Gamma\)-image factors are cancelled against the unfolded leg-parametrised
2830	  codomain read Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs} by the three one-cancellation
2831	  atoms. Carrying the legs as free parameters and identifying them with
2832	  \(e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\) definitionally removes the dependent-motive obstruction of
2833	  the literal-leg form.
2834	
2835	  \emph{The residual object-form diamond.} After distributing \(\Gamma\) over the composite, the
2836	  factor to be collapsed sits over the \emph{nested-image} object \(G(H(M))\) — the form produced
2837	  by distributing a functor over a composite of morphisms — whereas the collapsing atom fixes the
2838	  same object in its \emph{composed-functor} form \((H \circ G)(M)\), the form in which the
2839	  pushforward-composition coherence isomorphism presents its (co)domain. These two object
2840	  presentations are definitionally but not syntactically equal, creating a diamond that recurs at
2841	  each of the three cancellations.
2842	
2843	  \emph{The resolution: congruence lifting.} Each factor-equation is lifted into the ambient
2844	  composite \emph{one neighbour at a time}: a factor identity \(u = u'\) is promoted to
2845	  \(c \circ u = c \circ u'\), or to \(u \circ c = u' \circ c\), by congruence in the composition
2846	  operation with the neighbouring factor \(c\) held fixed, and the promoted steps are chained in
2847	  sequence. The chain is then closed on the definitional bridge between the nested-image and
2848	  composed-functor presentations of the shared object, absorbing the object-form diamond at that
2849	  single closing step. The three atoms
2850	  (Lemmas~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_eUnit},
2851	  \ref{lem:base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_pushforwardComp},
2852	  \ref{lem:base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_pullbackComp}) are supplied in the
2853	  \(\Gamma \circ (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_{*}\) form and are spliced directly into the composite
2854	  by this congruence chaining.
2855	
2856	  \emph{The three cancellations.} Cancellation~(1): the \(e\)-unit is invertible
2857	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_eUnit}) and meets its inverse inside the codomain
2858	  read. Cancellation~(2): the surviving pushforward-composition coherence has identity
2859	  \(\Gamma\)-image (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_pushforwardComp}, whose
2860	  section-level content is the collapse Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaMap_pushforwardComp_hom_eq_id}).
2861	  Cancellation~(3): the trailing pullback-comparison factor cancels the inverse the codomain read
2862	  bakes in (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_pullbackComp}). The lone survivor is the
2863	  affine \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)\)-unit, evaluated by Seam~1
2864	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}) to \(\rho\).
2865	\end{proof}

2888	  In the concrete pullback square (\(g' = \operatorname{pr}_1\), \(f' = \operatorname{pr}_2\)),
2889	  the inner composite \(\theta_{\mathrm{in}}\) of
2890	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_inner_unitReduce}, read on global sections over

2945	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base change''
2946	  (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}
2947	  Let \(\rho : M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\), \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\), be the
2948	  inner comparison value of Definition~\ref{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}. The extension of
2949	  scalars along \(\psi : R \to R'\) of \(\rho\), post-composed with the algebraic counit
2950	  \(\varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}\) of the extension/restriction-of-scalars adjunction along \(\psi\),
2951	  is an \(R'\)-linear map \(R' \otimes_R M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\); on the generator
2952	  \(r' \otimes m\) it returns \(r' \cdot ((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m) = (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\),
2953	  which is exactly the value of the inverse regrouping isomorphism
2954	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}) on \(r' \otimes m\). Both maps being
2955	  \(R'\)-linear and agreeing on the generators \(r' \otimes m\), they coincide:
2956	  \[
2957	    \operatorname{ext}_\psi(\rho) \,\circ\, \varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}
2958	      \;=\; \operatorname{regroup}^{-1}.
2959	  \]
2960	\end{lemma}
2961	\begin{proof}
2962	  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv, def:base_change_mate_inner_value}
2963	  Both sides are \(R'\)-linear maps out of \(R' \otimes_R M\), so by extensionality it suffices to
2964	  check the value on the generators \(r' \otimes m\). The left side sends
2965	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto r' \cdot \rho(m) = r' \cdot ((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m) =
2966	  (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\), using that \(\varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}\) reads the extension of
2967	  scalars by acting through the \(R'\)-scalar. The inverse regrouping isomorphism of
2968	  Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv} sends, by definition,
2969	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\) (in the \(A \otimes_R R'\) orientation). The two
2970	  values agree on every generator, so the maps are equal. No flatness hypothesis is used.
2971	\end{proof}

3788	    intersection of two quasi-compact opens is quasi-compact, so each \(U_{ij}\) is
3789	    quasi-compact. When \(X\) is separated the diagonal is a closed immersion, whence
3790	    the intersection of two affine opens is affine (Mathlib, for separated schemes).
